reasoning:
The exact baseline rating on 2025-04-13, according to the April 10 revision of the Wikipedia page "FIDE rankings", is 2758. A 1% increase requires him to achieve a rating of approximately 2786. Gaining 28 Elo points is a massive undertaking for a player already in the global top 10 (World #7), requiring repeated, dominant performances against elite Super GM competition. 

- For April 20, the probability is practically zero because FIDE only issues official lists on the 1st of each month; Wikipedia will still reflect the 2758 rating.
- For May 13, the rating will reflect his April performance, but gaining 28 points in a single month at this level is nearly unprecedented without a massive outlier tournament.
- For July 12, there is marginally more time (Grand Chess Tour events), but he would likely need to decisively win multiple super-tournaments. 
- For October 10, he has about six months of play. Talented juniors like Gukesh and Arjun Erigaisi have demonstrated that +30 point swings over 6-9 months are possible, so uncertainty increases to reflect his long-term steady rise, though it remains unlikely.
